question: A mobile phone retailer sells a phone for RsP and earns a profit of 20%. For a special festive offer, he marks the same phone at Rs1.5P. At the offer, he provides a discount of 10%. What will be the percentage profit that he will make during the festive offer?
A shopkeeper marked an item 25% above its cost price. During a sale, he offered a discount of 10%. What was his profit percentage?

A shopkeeper marks his goods at 60% above the cost price. He allows a discount of 25% on the marked price. If he also gives an additional cash discount of Rs150, and still makes a profit of 14% on the cost price, what is the cost price of the goods?

A wholesaler buys a batch of ceiling fans at a 50% markup over the factory's base cost RsC. He then allows a 20% trade discount to the retailer. The retailer applies a 40% markup on his cost and offers a flat 10% discount to customers on the printed price. If a customer finally pays Rs18,144, what is the original base cost (RsC) of one ceiling fan from the factory?

A bookseller sells a novel for RsY and makes a loss of 10%. To clear old stock, he decides to mark the novel at Rs0.8Y. He then allows a further discount of 5% on this marked price. What will be the percentage loss that he will incur on this clearance sale?
An article is marked at Rs800. A shop owner allows a discount of 12% and still gains 8%. What is the approximate cost price of the article?

A furniture manufacturer sells a dining table set to a showroom at a 25% discount on the marked price, but adds a 10% handling fee on the discounted price. The showroom then sells the set for Rs4500 more than what they paid, thereby earning a profit of 30%. At what price had the manufacturer marked the dining table set?

A wholesaler marks up the price of a microwave oven by 60% above its cost price. He gives a 15% trade discount to a retailer. The retailer, in turn, marks up the price by 25% above his purchase price and offers a 10% festival discount to the customer. If the customer finally pays Rs14,490 for the microwave, what is the original approximate cost price of the oven to the wholesaler?
A dress is marked at Rs3,500. A discount offer changes from 10% to 15%. How much extra discount does a customer get?
A dealer sets the marked price of a television 20% higher than its cost price. If he gives a discount of 15% and makes a profit of Rs300, what is the cost price of the television?

A merchant marked an article such that its price was 25% above its cost price. He offered two consecutive discounts of 10% and 20% to a buyer. If he incurred a loss of Rs400, what was the marked price of the article?

A furniture store sells a dining table for RsZ, making a 25% profit. During a festive season, they increase the marked price of the same table to Rs1.6Z. They then offer a special discount of 20% on this increased marked price. What will be the percentage profit made by the store during the festive season?

A book publisher offers a novel to a bookstore at a 30% discount on the cover price. However, they charge an additional 5% on the discounted price for shipping. The bookstore sells the novel for Rs1620 more, earning a profit of 40%. What was the original cover price of the novel?

A garment shop sells a shirt for Rs A, incurring a loss of 5%. During an end-of-season sale, they mark up the same shirt to Rs 1.2A. At the sale, they provide a flat discount of 10% on this marked price. What will be the percentage profit or loss for the garment shop on that shirt during the sale?

A shopkeeper marked an item 50% above its cost price. He then offered two successive discounts of 10% and 20% to a customer. If he made a profit of Rs80, at what price did he sell the item to that customer?

A high-end watch is initially marked up by 50% above its cost price. During a sale, it is offered at a discount of 20% on its marked price. However, a special customer receives an additional discount of y% on the discounted price, bringing the final selling price to Rs4320. If the shopkeeper still makes a profit of 8% on the cost price after all discounts, what was the original cost price of the watch and the value of y?

A television set has a marked price of Rs 20,000. It is sold after two successive discounts. If the first discount is 15% and the final selling price is Rs 15,300, what is the percentage of the second discount?
A manufacturer marked a product 30% above its cost price. He provided a discount to a customer, which resulted in him making a profit of 17%. What was the rate of discount given by the manufacturer?

A mobile phone retailer sells a phone for RsP and earns a profit of 20%. For a special festive offer, he marks the same phone at Rs1.5P. At the offer, he provides a discount of 10%. What will be the percentage profit that he will make during the festive offer?

A gadget is sold at a 15% discount on its marked price. This sale leads to a profit of 20% on its cost price. If the profit earned is Rs450, what would have been the selling price if the gadget was sold at a 25% discount on its marked price?
An item is marked at Rs150. A customer gets a 10% discount on it. If the seller still makes a profit of 25%, what is the cost price of the item?

A software developer sells a license for a new application to a reseller at a 20% discount. An installation and support fee of 15% is added to the discounted price. The reseller then sells the license for Rs7800 more, making a 25% profit. Determine the original marked price of the software license by the developer.
An oven has a marked price of Rs 12,000. It is sold with two successive discounts. If the second discount is 20% and the final selling price is Rs 8,400, what is the percentage of the first discount?

A furniture store marks its items at 80% above the cost price. They offer a discount of 30% on the marked price. If a customer receives an additional loyalty discount of Rs250, and the store still makes a 12% profit on the cost price, what is the cost price of the furniture item?
The cost price of an article is Rs500. A shopkeeper sells it at a discount of 20% on the marked price. If the marked price is Rs600, what is the shopkeeper's profit or loss percentage?
A shopkeeper marked a watch at Rs800. He sold it after allowing a 15% discount. If his profit on the sale was 20%, what was the cost price of the watch?

A product is sold at a 20% discount on its marked price. This transaction results in a profit of 10% on its cost price. If the profit earned is Rs300, what would have been the selling price if the product was sold at a 30% discount on its marked price?
A pair of shoes is listed at Rs 800. After two successive discounts, it is sold for Rs 612. If the first discount offered was 10%, what was the second discount percentage?
A retailer marked a product 60% above its cost price. He provided two successive discounts of 25% and 10%. If he earned a profit of Rs160, what was the cost price of the product?

A laptop is priced at Rs40,000. A customer gets a discount of Rs4,000 on it. After the discount, the customer buys a mouse for Rs1,500 at a 20% discount. What is the total amount the customer spends on both items?

A book, originally priced at Rs600, is subject to a reduction in cost through two consecutive discounts: an initial markdown of 10%, followed by an additional 20% discount. The question then is to determine the conclusive selling price of the book once both these successive price adjustments have been applied.

Having initially established the marked price of a particular commodity at a 20% premium over its cost price, a retail vendor subsequently extended an unspecified discount. If, following this concession, the transaction ultimately yielded a profit of 8% on the cost price, what was the exact percentage of the discount given?
A trader marked an article 40% above its cost price. He then allowed a discount of 20% on the marked price. If the selling price of the article was Rs560, what was its cost price?

A furniture establishment initially disposes of a particular dining table for RsZ, thereby realising a 25% profit margin. Subsequently, as a promotional strategy for a festive period, the marked price of this identical table is elevated to Rs1.6Z. Following this adjustment, a special concession of 20% is applied to the newly established marked price. The inquiry then seeks to ascertain the resulting percentage profit generated by the store during this specific celebratory sales period. What is the percentage profit earned by the store during the festive sale?

A software company developed 800 licenses for a new application at a total cost of Rs4,000,000. They provided 50 licenses free to their top beta testers. For the remaining licenses, they offered a 15% discount on the market price of Rs6000 per license. Additionally, they gave 1 license free for every 9 licenses bought. If all 800 licenses were distributed, what is the company's overall gain or loss percentage?

A bakery produced 5000 cakes at a total cost of Rs250,000. They gave away 500 cakes for a promotional event. For the remaining cakes, they offered a 20% discount on the market price of Rs75 per cake. Additionally, they gave one cake free with every 9 cakes bought. If all 5000 cakes were distributed, what is their overall gain or loss percentage?

A wholesaler sells a packet of tea to a retailer at a profit of 20%. The retailer then marks up the price by 50% and offers a discount of 20% to the customer. If the customer pays Rs. 480, find the cost price of the packet for the wholesaler.

An electronics dealer marks an item 60% above cost and offers a festival discount of 25%, plus a cashback of Rs. 300 on the final selling price. If he still makes a 10% profit, what is the cost price?
A person bought a cycle at a discount of 20% on the marked price. He spent Rs. 400 on repairs and sold it for Rs. 4,800, gaining 25%. What was the marked price?
A seller offers a 40% discount on an item whose cost price is Rs. 720. Despite the discount, the seller earns a profit of 20%. What must be the marked price?
A computer is sold at Rs. 7650 after applying a 15% discount. What was its market price?

A store announces a festive deal: "Get 30% off on purchases above Rs. 10,000, and an additional Rs. 1,000 off if the total after discount exceeds Rs. 15,000." A customer buys items worth Rs. 25,000. How much does the customer finally pay?
A seller offers a combo deal: "Buy 3 shirts for the price of 2". If each shirt costs him Rs. 300, what is the effective loss percentage?

A wholesaler offers a bulk order deal where a 10% discount is applied to orders above Rs. 15,000, and a 5% additional seasonal discount is available. What is the effective price paid for an order worth Rs. 20,000?
A dealer sells a packet of cookies at Rs. 120 after giving a 25% discount on the marked price. If the profit earned is 20%, find the cost price.

A wholesaler marks up a batch of 100 identical clocks by 80% on cost and offers a trade discount of 25% to retailers. A retailer purchases all the clocks and spends Rs. 48,000. What is the cost price per clock for the wholesaler?
A trader gave a 20% discount on the market price but still earned a 10% profit. What percentage was the market price above the cost price?
A combo offer says, "Buy 2 shirts at Rs. 1200 each, get a third shirt free." What is the effective price per shirt?
A seller gives a 20% discount and charges 18% GST on the discounted price. On a Rs. 2000 item, how much is payable?
A shopkeeper offers a festival deal, "Flat Rs. 150 off on every purchase above Rs. 1000." What is the effective discount percentage if a buyer purchases an item marked Rs. 1200?

A bookshop advertises: "Buy 4 books and get the 5th at 50% discount." If the marked price of each book is Rs. 480 and all books are priced the same, what is the effective percentage discount the customer receives on the total purchase?
If the price tag of a microwave is Rs. 5000 and the shopkeeper offers a discount of 18%, what is the selling price?

A departmental store marked up the price of an electronic gadget by 60% above its cost price. During a clearance sale, it offered two successive discounts of 25% and 10%. If the final selling price was Rs. 2,430, what was the cost price of the gadget?
A buyer gets two successive discounts of 20% and 10% on a Rs. 1000 item. What is the price paid?
A trader marks his goods 40% above the cost price and gives a discount of 25%. If he gains Rs. 75 on an item, what is the cost price of the item?
An online retailer offers a Rs. 150 discounts on an item originally priced at Rs. 1200. What percentage discount is being offered?
A shopkeeper marks an article at Rs. 720 and gives a discount of Rs. 144. He earns a profit of Rs. 36. What is the cost price?
A store offers a "25% off" discount but charges 5% VAT on the discounted price. If the marked price is Rs. 1600, what is the final amount paid?

In a scheme, a shopkeeper offers a 10% discount if you buy two or more books. Kiran buys two books for Rs. 540. If the shopkeeper still makes a 20% profit, find the profit he would make had he sold the books without the discount. [SSC CHSL 2025 Tier 1, 12 Nov Shift 3]
The printed price of a TV set is Rs10,000. After two consecutive discounts, it is sold for Rs8,100. What is the second discount if the first one is 10%? [SSC CHSL 2025 Tier 1, 13 Nov Shift 1]

A jeweler marks a necklace at 30% above its cost price. She gives a cash discount of Rs150 on the marked price. If the necklace is sold for Rs1800 and she earns a profit of 20%, what is the cost price of the necklace? [SSC CHSL Tier 1, 13 Nov 2025, Shift-2]

In a sale, a shopkeeper offers a 15% discount on clothes if you buy more than two items. Priya buys four shirts for Rs2040. If the shopkeeper still makes a 20% profit, find the profit the shopkeeper would make if he had sold the clothes at full price.

A jeweler marks a necklace at 30% above its cost price. She gives a cash discount of Rs150 on the marked price. If the necklace is sold for Rs1800 and she earns a profit of 20%, what is the cost price of the necklace? [SSC CHSL Tier 1, 13 Nov 2025, Shift-2]
A retailer marks his goods at 40% above the cost price and allows a 15% discount. What is his profit percentage?
12 books, each of Rs. 360, are sold at Rs. 3,600. What is the percentage of the discount given? [SSC CHSL Tier 1, 15 Nov 2025, Shift-1]

A vintage clock was bought for Rs10,000. Its price was marked up by 40%. Thereafter, it was sold at a discount of 10% on the marked price. What was the percentage profit on the transaction? [SSC CHSL Tier 1, 15 Nov 2025, Shift-1]
Sheela gets a discount of 25% on Rs. 10000 oven. Since she pays cash, she gets an additional 2% discount too. How much does she pay?
The selling price of a vintage vase is Rs. 1800, and the discount given is 4%. What is the marked price of the vase?
A vase has a marked price of Rs. 550. Simran pays Rs. 350 for it because she got 2 successive discounts, one of 20% and the other of:
A seller marks his goods 32% above their cost price but allows a 16% discount for cash payment. His percentage of profit when sold in cash is:

An item undergoes two successive discounts: 12.5% (i.e., 1/8) and then 20%. After those discounts, a tax of 18% is applied on the reduced price, and a customer pays is Rs927. What was the original price of the item? (Give exact value and a rounded amount to two decimal places)
The price of a laptop is increased by 25% and then a discount of 20% is offered on the new price. If the final price becomes Rs48,000, what was the original price?

A shopkeeper offers a 20% discount on the marked price of an article. If the marked price of the article is Rs2,500 and the shopkeeper further gives a 10% discount on the already reduced price, what is the final selling price of the article?
A shopkeeper offers two successive discounts of 10% and 20% on a laptop priced at Rs40,000. What is the final selling price after both discounts?

A shopkeeper sells an article at a discount of 12%. Had he given only a 5% discount, he would have earned Rs504 more as profit. If the cost price of the article is Rs3,000, then find the marked price.

The marked prices of a pen and pencil are in the ratio of 5:6. The shopkeeper gives an 18% discount on the pen. If the total discount on both the pen and the pencil is 12%, then the discount offered on the pencil is:
A shopkeeper marks up his goods by 20% and then offers a discount of 10%. If the cost price is Rs500, what is the selling price?
If the original price of the items is Rs 2,050 and the discount is 26%, what will be the final price after the discount?
A microwave is sold at a discount of 25%. If a man buys it for Rs. 7500, what is the marked price?
A watch is sold for Rs840 after a discount of 20% is applied. What was the original marked price of the watch?
If A purchases items worth Rs2,050 at a discount rate of 26%, what will be his total savings?
After deducting 25% from the marked price, the item's selling price is Rs7,200. If the discount is raised to 30%, what will the selling price be?
An article marked at Rs. 1600 is sold for Rs. 1360. Find the discount percentage.
A shopkeeper offers successive discounts of 20% and 10% on a bag and sells it for Rs864. What was the original marked price of the bag?
A product is sold for Rs1,680 after a 16% discount is given on its marked price. What was the marked price of the product?
A shopkeeper offers two successive discounts of 20% and 10% on an item. If the marked price of the item is Rs1,200, what is its final selling price?
A shopkeeper offers two successive discounts of 25% and 10% on an item with a marked price of Rs1,200. What is the effective discount percentage offered to the customer?
A product is sold for Rs1,680 after a discount of 16% is given on its marked price. What would be the selling price if the discount was 25%?
The marked price of a cupboard is Rs3,200, which is 25% above the cost price. It is sold at a discount of 10% on the marked price. Find the profit percentage.
A shopkeeper provides two consecutive discounts of 20% and 10% on an item. Calculate the single discount rate that is equivalent to these successive discounts.
A TV is marked at Rs. 15,000. A discount of 10% is offered. Find the selling price.
Marked price of a watch is Rs. 1,200. If a discount of 25% is given, what is the selling price?
A product is marked at Rs. 8000 and sold at a discount of 15%. What is the selling price?
By selling a watch at 80% of its marked price, a shopkeeper loses 4%. What would have been his profit percentage if he had sold it at 95% of the marked price?
The cost price of a TV set is Rs 14000, and its marked price is Rs 16000. If the shopkeeper sells it at a gain of 8%, then what is the percentage rate of discount offered by him?
At a shopping center, a 50% discount is offered on all its items. What would be the sale price of a shoe marked at Rs. 5200?
If a shopkeeper gives 10% discount on Rs. 2,000, what is the amount of discount?
A merchant marks his goods at 100% above the cost price and offers two successive discounts of 30% and 20%. Find the profit percentage.
A table is marked at Rs. 20,000 is sold at Rs. 14,000. What is the percentage of discount given?
If a book costs Rs. 800 and is sold with a profit of 10% and discount of 12% then the marked price is:
A discount of 5% is given on an article priced at Rs. 2,000. Find the selling price.
The value of discount given on an article is (2/17) of the marked price. What is the ratio of the value of discount to the selling price?
A product marked at Rs. 1,500 is sold for Rs. 1,200. Find the discount percentage.
An item is sold at 25% discount. If the marked price is Rs. 800, what is the selling price?
What is the discount amount on a Rs. 1,200 item sold at 15% discount?
Selling price of an article is 120 percent more than the value of discount. Selling price is what percent of the marked price?
A product marked Rs. 5,000 is sold at 20% discount. Find the selling price.
Profit of Ravindra while selling a book to Meeta is Rs. 68, Despite a discount of 15% on the book, Ravindra managed to get a 10% profit. What must be the list price of the book?
A merchant gives a series of 3 successive discounts of 5%, 10%, and 20% on an item priced at Rs 1,500. What is the final price after the discounts?
An article sold for Rs. 765 after a discount of 15%. What was the marked price?

A trader marks up an article by 25% on its cost price. He then offers two successive discounts: 10% followed by 5% on the marked price. If the cost price (CP) is Rs480, what is the final profit or loss percent?

A retailer sells a product for RsA, incurring a loss of 10%. During a sale, he marks up the product by 17.5% on the cost price and provides a flat discount of 20% on the marked price. What is the percentage profit or loss for the retailer during the sale?
A microwave oven has a marked price of Rs8,000. It sells for Rs6,200 during the sale season. What is the offered percentage discount?
Marked price of an article is 140 percent of the value of discount. Marked price is what percent of the selling price?
A dealer allows 10% discount and still gains 8%. Find the marked price if the cost price is Rs. 500.
A shopkeeper offers two successive discounts of 10% and 20% on a Rs. 1,200 article. What is the final price after the two discounts?
Marked price of an article is 150 percent of the value of discount. Marked price is what percent of the selling price?
Marked price of an article is 140 percent more than the value of discount. Selling price is what percent of the marked price?
A 25% discount on an article brings the price to Rs. 600. What is the original price?
A shopkeeper wants to give a single discount equivalent to two successive discounts of 15% and 10%. What is the single equivalent discount?
What is the selling price if the marked price is Rs. 750 and discount is 12%?
A single discount of 25% on the marked price of an item is equivalent to two successive discounts of 20% and x%. Find the value of x.
A shopkeeper provides a 20% discount on the marked price and still achieves a 25% profit. If the selling price of the article is Rs1500, what is the marked price of the article?
Selling price of an article is 140 percent of the value of discount. Marked price is what percent of the value of discount?
A shirt is sold for 840 after a discount of 16% is offered on its marked price. What was the original marked price of the shirt?
A store offers three successive discounts of 20%, 10%, and 5% on a shirt. What is the single equivalent discount?
An article is sold at Rs. 920 after giving a discount of 8%. What is the marked price?
If a 20% discount is given on Rs. 2,500, what is the selling price?
A shopkeeper offers a 'Buy 3, Get 1 Free' scheme. What is the equivalent single discount percentage?

A fan's cost price is unknown. A retailer marks it up so that its marked price is Rs. 5,000. They offer a 20% discount on this marked price. Even after the discount, they make a profit of 25%. What was the original cost price of the fan for the retailer? [SSC CHSL 2025 Tier 1, 12 Nov Shift 2]
An article is marked at Rs2,400. A shopkeeper allows successive discounts of 25% and 10%. What is the final selling price?
A mobile phone was sold for Rs. 12,600 after allowing a 10% discount on the marked price. If the profit was 20%, what was the cost price?
A seller marks up his product by 50% and offers two consecutive discounts of 10% each. What is his profit percentage?
A retailer offers 2 successive discounts of 15% and 10% on a product marked at Rs. 1,000. If his cost price is Rs. 700, what is his profit or loss percentage?
A laptop is listed at Rs. 60,000. If a seller offers a festival discount of 20% followed by a cashback of Rs. 2,000, what is the effective price paid?
A retailer marked a jacket 60% above its cost price. During an off-season clearance, he offered a flat discount of Rs. 1800 and still made a profit of 20%. What was the cost price of the jacket?
A trader buys an item at Rs. 500 and marks it up by 60%. If he then offers a 25% discount on the marked price, what is his profit percentage?
A trader offers a 30% discount on the marked price and still makes a 5% profit. If the item costs him Rs. 2100, what is the marked price?
If a customer gets a discount of Rs. 144 on a product originally priced at Rs. 960, and is later charged 18% GST on the discounted price, what is the final bill amount?
A store advertises, "Buy items worth Rs. 3000, get Rs. 500 off instantly." What is the effective discount percentage?
A notebook is marked at Rs50. The shopkeeper allows a flat discount of Rs5. What is the selling price?
An article is marked at Rs900. If a 30% discount is given, what is the selling price?
A shopkeeper marks an item at 1000 and allows a 20% discount. What is the selling price?
A bag is marked at Rs400 and is sold at a 5% discount. What is the selling price?
A shopkeeper marks goods 40% above cost price and offers a 20% discount on the marked price. What is his profit percentage?
A pen is marked at Rs100. The shopkeeper first gives a 5% discount, then another 5% discount. What is the final selling price?
An article is marked at Rs. 400. If sold at a 10% discount, what is the selling price ?
A shopkeeper marks an article at Rs 240 and offers a 25% discount. What is the selling price?
A clerk buys a dictionary marked at Rs. 300. The shopkeeper gives him a 10% discount. What is the selling price?
An item is marked at Rs. 1200 but sold for Rs. 960. What is the discount percentage?
An article costing Rs. 500 is sold with a Rs. 50 discount. What is the discount percentage?
A shopkeeper marks an item 50% above cost and gives a 20% discount. What is the net profit percentage?
An item is sold after successive discounts of 30% and 10%. What is the equivalent single discount percentage?
An article is marked at Rs1000 but sold for Rs850. What is the discount percentage?
A calculator is marked at Rs400 but sold for Rs360. What is the discount percentage?
Find the rate of discount when the marked price is Rs. 3500 and the selling price is Rs. 3150.
An item is marked at Rs. 2000 and sold after two successive discounts of 20% and 10%. What is the selling price?
A cloth is marked at Rs. 600 but sold for Rs. 540. What is the discount percentage?
A watch is listed for 1500. The store is giving a 25% discount. Calculate how much money the customer pays.
An item is sold with two successive discounts of 20% and 10%. What is the equivalent single discount percentage?
Maria bought a handbag marked at 4800. If the discount given is 12%, find the amount she paid.
A bag is marked at Rs750. If sold at a 10% discount, what is the selling price?
A trader sets a price 40% above cost and offers a 20% discount. What is the profit percentage?
An item has a marked price of Rs1500. If sold with a 12% discount, what is the selling price?
A jacket costs 3500. The shop offers a 15% discount. How much money does the customer pay to the cashier?
A shopkeeper sets a price 20% above cost and gives a 10% discount. What is the net gain percentage?
Ricky purchased a shirt for $2000. The discount offered for the shirt is 10%. Find how many dollars has he given to the cashier?
Find a single discount equal to three consecutive discounts of 9%, 14%, and 7%.
A trader buys an article at Rs800, marks it at Rs1000, and allows a 10% discount. What is his profit percentage?
An article is marked at Rs500 but sold for Rs400. What is the discount percentage?
An article is sold after successive discounts of 25% and 15%. What is the equivalent single discount percentage?
An article is marked at Rs600. A discount of Rs120 is given. What is the discount percentage?
An article is marked at and sold after successive discounts of and . What is the final selling price?
A shirt has a marked price of 800. It is sold after a 20% reduction. What is the selling price?
An article is marked at Rs1500. If successive discounts of 10% and 5% are given, what is the selling price?
An article is marked at Rs2000. If sold with successive discounts of 10% and 20%, what is the selling price?
Goods cost Rs500. The trader marks them 40% above cost and then gives a 25% discount. What is the selling price?
A shopkeeper offers two successive discounts of 10% each. What is the net equivalent discount percentage?
A trader marks goods 60% above the cost price and gives a 25% discount. What is his profit percentage?
A car is sold at Rs. 540000 after two successive discounts of 20% and 25%. Find the marked price.
A retailer allows a discount of 34% on the marked price and still gains 10%. If the cost price is Rs. 6000, what is the marked price?

A boutique proprietor increases the price of a designer jacket by 60% above its original cost. During a festival promotion, a 25% discount is applied to this marked price. If the jacket's cost price is Rs5,000, calculate the final selling price and the total profit percentage earned.
A sofa set marked at Rs. 78,000 is sold at Rs. 65,000. What is the percentage of discount?

A store selling mobile accessories has a special offer on screen guards: when a customer buys two screen guards priced at Rs 400 each, they receive a 5% discount on the total bill. How much does the customer have to pay after the discount is applied?

Ananya runs a clothing store and has a marked price of Rs12,000 for a winter coat, She offers two successive discounts: 12% and 18%. Ananya wants to know what single equivalent discount would result in the same final selling price as the two successive discounts. What is the single equivalent discount Ananya should offer to achieve the same final price as the two successive discounts?
A bicycle is marked at Rs15,000. The shopkeeper offers a 10% discount, and the bicycle is sold with a 20% profit on cost price. Find the cost price of the bicycle.

Rajesh has bought a watch for Rs5,000 from a local store and plans to sell it to a friend at a 20% discount on the marked price. Rajesh wants to make a 25% profit on the cost price after the discount. How much will he sell it for?
A smartwatch originally priced at Rs6,000 is now being sold at a 25% discount. What is the sale price?
During a festival sale, a boutique provides a discount on kurtas originally priced at . What will be the selling price after applying the discount?

80 40 एक वस्तु का अंकित मूल्य उसके क्रय मूल्य से प्रतिशत अधिक है। यदि अंकित मूल्य पर प्रतिशत की छूट दी , ? जाती है तो लाभ प्रतिशत क्या होगा En: The marked price of an article is 80 percent more than its cost price. If 40 percent discount is given on the marked price, then what will be the profit percentage? प्रतिशत प्रतिशत प्रतिशत प्रतिशत

4500 ? निम्नलिखित में से किस में अंकित मूल्य रुपये होगा En: Which of the following will have marked price Rs. 4500? विक्रय मूल्य रुपये तथा छूट प्रतिशत विक्रय मूल्य रुपये तथा छूट प्रतिशत विक्रय मूल्य रुपये तथा छूट प्रतिशत विक्रय मूल्य रुपये तथा छूट प्रतिशत

25 18 , किसी वस्तु का अंकित मूल्य उसके क्रय मूल्य से प्रतिशत अधिक है। यदि अर्जित लाभ प्रतिशत है तो वस्तु पर ? कितनी छूट दी गई है En: The marked price of an article is 25 percent more than its cost price.
